text:
REGIME — Narrow green surface masking rotation; small caps held, staples and housing cracked.

INDICES — VIX 25.8, elevated but off 52-week high of 52.3. IJR +0.33%, IWM +0.22%, IJH +0.13%, SPY +0.04%, QQQ +0.02%, DIA -0.19%; small caps outperformed large caps across the board. VIXY surged +3.52%, signaling latent fear beneath a flat tape.

SECTORS — XLE +0.80%, XLK +0.48%, XLU +0.22%, XLV +0.20%, XLC +0.05%, XLF +0.00%, XLRE -0.10%, XLI -0.20%, XLB -0.28%, XLY -1.16%, XLP -1.69%. Top industries: WGMI +2.37%, XES +2.08%; bottom: XHB -2.23%, MSOS -8.56%.

THE 7s — Buildout +1.55%, Semi +1.43%, Old Metals +1.14%, Fab +0.79%, Energy +0.71%, Health +0.53%, Power +0.36%, Cloud +0.33%, Bank +0.23%, Industrial +0.07%, New Staples -0.06%, Insurance -0.10%, Freight -0.18%, Software -0.25%, Medtech -0.25%, Toll Road -0.35%, Defense -1.02%, New Metals -1.03%, Old Staples -1.90%, Housing -1.91%. Strongest ticker in Buildout 7 led the session; weakest in Housing 7 extended its -6.53% 20-day skid.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 54.2, Neutral. Top movers: UNH +9.37% (Health, 2.5x vol spike), MSOS -8.56% (Industries), WGMI +2.37% (Industries). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +7.52%, worst Software 7 -9.85%; best basket Energy +9.02%, worst Software -9.85%. XLE gained +0.80% today despite USO falling -0.62%, as Middle East de-escalation signals pressured crude but lifted energy equities on margin relief expectations.

WATCH — UNH +9.37% (2.5x vol): gap size and volume demand follow-through confirmation before chasing. MSOS -8.56%: sector-wide collapse warrants watching for contagion into consumer discretionary tomorrow. XLP -1.69%: worst sector on a risk-neutral day; 20d trend -4.95% accelerating — defensive rotation thesis breaking down.
